description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities through documented review criteria, corrective action expectations, and escalation for missed or violated control obligations.

subcriteria.name: accountability_criteria
subcriteria.reference_text: A review rubric, performance standard, or manager guidance document shows that control responsibilities are part of employee or role accountability. Real evidence often mentions policy compliance, control ownership, remediation deadlines, escalation discipline, acknowledgment records, or rating impact when responsibilities are overdue or missed.

subcriteria.name: corrective_action_and_escalation
subcriteria.reference_text: The document explains what happens when internal control responsibilities are not met. Good evidence often says repeated missed deadlines trigger a documented coaching conversation, follow-up review, corrective action plan, escalation to a manager or functional leader, or referral to HR for serious violations.

subcriteria.name: documented_follow_up
subcriteria.reference_text: A strong accountability artifact shows follow-up is documented rather than only encouraged. Real evidence often mentions review sign-off, remediation tickets, completion dates, follow-up within a defined time period, acknowledgment records, or performance-system tracking of corrective actions.

mismatch_signals.reason: Recognition or compensation content alone is not evidence of accountability for control responsibilities.
Technical monitoring evidence is not personnel accountability evidence.
